name: MoneyGram
description_short: MoneyGram is a money transfer and payment service with 19+ locations across Omaha, Nebraska, offering domestic and international money sending and receiving through retail partner locations.
description_long:
MoneyGram is a global money transfer company that operates through a network of retail agent locations rather than physical branch offices. The company has established partnerships with major retailers and local businesses to offer financial services to consumers who need convenient access to money transfer and payment solutions. In the Omaha, Nebraska market, MoneyGram operates through 19 documented locations as of the data provided, partnering with chains like Walmart and CVS as well as local businesses such as Order Express and Maya Market. This partnership model allows MoneyGram to achieve widespread geographic coverage without maintaining its own storefront infrastructure, making services available in neighborhoods across the metro area including south Omaha, west Omaha, and north Omaha locations.

MoneyGram's primary service offering is money transfer—both domestic transfers within the United States and international transfers to recipients globally. Customers can send and receive money through either online channels or in-person at physical agent locations. The service emphasizes ease of use and accessibility, with the ability to manage transfers through multiple channels. Based on the website structure and standard industry practices for check-cashing category companies, MoneyGram also provides related financial services such as money orders, bill payment processing, and wire transfer capabilities, though specific service details beyond money transfer are not extensively detailed on the location-search page reviewed.

MoneyGram's primary distinction is its massive retail network distribution model, with partnerships spanning Walmart stores, CVS pharmacies, and local independent retailers. This approach provides substantially greater geographic accessibility and extended hours compared to traditional financial institutions—many Omaha locations remain open until 8:00 PM or 9:00 PM, accommodating working customers who cannot visit banks during standard business hours. The integration into existing retail spaces means customers can accomplish money transfers while shopping or during convenient shopping trips. The company's emphasis on both online and offline transfer capabilities provides flexibility for different customer preferences and technological comfort levels.

Honestly assessed, MoneyGram operates in the check-cashing and money services category, which typically involves higher fees than traditional banking for financial transactions. While the convenience and accessibility are genuine advantages for unbanked and underbanked consumers, customers should be aware that money transfer fees apply and vary by location and transfer type. The reliance on third-party retail agents means service quality and staff knowledge may vary by location. For consumers with access to traditional banking, bank-to-bank transfers are typically more economical; MoneyGram's value proposition centers on accessibility and convenience rather than cost efficiency.
pros: 19 documented locations across Omaha with extended evening hours (many open until 8:00-9:00 PM)
Partnerships with major retailers like Walmart, CVS, and local businesses increase accessibility
Dual online and offline transfer options accommodate different customer preferences
Serves both domestic US transfers and international money sending capabilities
Locations in diverse Omaha neighborhoods including south, west, and north areas
No requirement for bank account to send or receive money transfers
Immediate availability at retail partner locations without needing to travel to dedicated offices
cons: MoneyGram charges transaction fees for money transfers that vary by location and transfer amount
Service quality and staff expertise may vary significantly between different retail agent locations
Limited service details provided on location-search website; specifics on fee structure not disclosed
Domestic bank transfers through traditional banks are typically more cost-effective than MoneyGram services
Reliance on third-party retailers means service availability depends on host business hours and priorities
best_for: Unbanked and underbanked consumers without access to traditional bank accounts
Individuals needing to send money to family members internationally or domestically
Customers seeking convenient money transfer services during evening or weekend hours
People preferring to complete financial transactions while shopping at retail partners
diagnosis: MoneyGram is best for unbanked/underbanked consumers and those needing convenient money transfers during extended retail hours, particularly for international remittances. The main caveat is that MoneyGram charges transaction fees making it less cost-effective than traditional bank transfers—the value proposition centers on accessibility and convenience for those without banking alternatives, not economic efficiency.
services: Domestic money transfer within the United States
International money transfer to recipients globally
In-person money transfer at retail agent locations
Cash pickup for money received transfers
